SEARCH
Package de.uka.ilkd.key.wd

Class StatementWellDefinedness

java.lang.Object
de.uka.ilkd.key.wd.WellDefinednessCheck
de.uka.ilkd.key.wd.StatementWellDefinedness
All Implemented Interfaces:
Contract, SpecificationElement
Direct Known Subclasses:
BlockWellDefinedness, LoopWellDefinedness
public abstract class StatementWellDefinedness extends WellDefinednessCheck
A contract for checking the well-definedness of a jml statement. Contrary to jml specifications for methods, model fields and class invariants, the well-definedness of jml statements is checked "on-the-fly", i.e. before/when it is applied, since the check needs the context it is placed in. Thus the technical difference to other well-definedness checks is the context update and that it is not a separate contract, but another branch of the specific rule of the overlying proof. The according proof sequent is built in generateSequent(de.uka.ilkd.key.wd.StatementWellDefinedness.SequentTerms, de.uka.ilkd.key.logic.TermServices) Nevertheless it is imaginable to make them separate contracts.

  • Method Details

    • getStatement

      public abstract SpecificationElement getStatement()

    • generateSequent

      public SequentFormula generateSequent(LocationVariable self, LocationVariable exception, LocationVariable result, LocationVariable heap, LocationVariable heapAtPre, JTerm anonHeap, ImmutableSet<LocationVariable> ps, JTerm leadingUpdate, JTerm localAnonUpdate, Services services)
      This is where the proof sequent is built.
      Parameters:
      self - The current self variable
      exception - The current exception variable
      result - The current result variable
      heap - The current heap
      heapAtPre - The current old heap
      anonHeap - The anonymized heap
      ps - The current parameter variables
      leadingUpdate - The context update
      localAnonUpdate - anonymization update of local variables
      services - The current services reference
      Returns:
      The proof sequent for the well-definedness check

    • generateSequent

      public SequentFormula generateSequent(LocationVariable self, LocationVariable heap, JTerm anonHeap, ImmutableSet<LocationVariable> ps, JTerm leadingUpdate, JTerm localAnonUpdate, Services services)
      This is where the proof sequent is built, however with a smaller set of variables, due to the nature of the jml statement. This means no exception variable, no result variable and variable for the heap of the pre-state.
      Parameters:
      self - self variable
      heap - heap variable
      anonHeap - anonymised heap
      ps - set of parameter variables
      leadingUpdate - the context update
      localAnonUpdate - anonymization update of local variables
      services -
      Returns:
      The proof sequent for the well-definedness check
